question: Should Mullgate pursue a shared-entry redesign that reuses one Mullvad WireGuard identity for many concurrent country-distinct exits?

recommendation.summary: Do not pursue the shared-entry redesign as-is because the compatibility verifier found blocked contracts that prevent R028 from advancing truthfully.
rationale: SOCKS5 chaining can still work with explicit relay selection, but hostname-selected routing fails and HTTP/HTTPS cannot keep Mullgate’s current truthful contract: Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection.

Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection.
The redesign is blocked by failed requirements: R004, R006, R007, R017.

requirement.title: Many concurrent country-distinct exits on one Mullvad account
requirement.summary: R028 is blocked because Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection. Supporting failures: R004, R006, R007, R017.

compatibility.headline: SOCKS5 chaining can still work with explicit relay selection, but hostname-selected routing fails and HTTP/HTTPS cannot keep Mullgate’s current truthful contract: Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection.
compatibility.hostnameRoutingFailure: Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection.

protocolMatrix.summary: SOCKS5 only works if operators or clients explicitly choose the Mullvad relay, so the product contract would need to change.
protocolMatrix.observedCapabilitySummary: SOCKS5 chaining reaches distinct Mullvad exits only when the client explicitly chooses a relay hostname for the second hop.

protocolMatrix.summary: HTTP cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
protocolMatrix.observedCapabilitySummary: HTTP reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

protocolMatrix.summary: HTTPS cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
protocolMatrix.observedCapabilitySummary: HTTPS reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

notes: Authenticated access remains on the shared entry.
Operators can inspect relay mapping in the compatibility bundle, but clients must opt into explicit relay choice.

supportingRequirements.title: Hostname-selected location routing
supportingRequirements.summary: Hostname-selected routing no longer truthfully selects the Mullvad exit under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: Hostname-selected routing is not truthful under one bind IP because SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for the current destination-bind-IP selector.

supportingRequirements.title: SOCKS5 support
supportingRequirements.summary: SOCKS5 only works if operators or clients explicitly choose the Mullvad relay, so the product contract would need to change.
evidence: SOCKS5 chaining reaches distinct Mullvad exits only when the client explicitly chooses a relay hostname for the second hop.

supportingRequirements.title: HTTP proxy support
supportingRequirements.summary: HTTP cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: HTTP reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

supportingRequirements.title: HTTPS proxy support
supportingRequirements.summary: HTTPS cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: HTTPS reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

supportingRequirements.title: Authenticated proxy access
supportingRequirements.summary: Authenticated proxy access remains visible and enforced at the shared entry.
evidence: Shared-entry probing still requires authenticated local proxy access.

supportingRequirements.title: Low-friction first run for self-hosters
supportingRequirements.summary: The shared-entry topology breaks the low-friction self-hoster story because at least one required protocol no longer works truthfully.
evidence: Operators must configure client-specific relay selection instead of relying on hostname-only routing.

supportingRequirements.title: Easy location naming and discovery
supportingRequirements.summary: Route naming remains diagnosable, but operators must understand an explicit relay-selection mapping instead of hostname-only routing.
evidence: Hostname-selected routing is not truthful under one bind IP because SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for the current destination-bind-IP selector.
Location-selection metadata can still be described to operators in a compatibility bundle.

notes: Fixture proves that one-entry SOCKS5 chaining can still reach distinct exits while hostname-selected routing fails.
HTTP and HTTPS remain explicit contract failures because no truthful relay selector survives the one-entry topology.
Fixture replay mode skipped all live Mullvad and Docker prerequisites.
